data.title: Wall Street rises as markets continue to shrug off shutdown concerns
data.description:
The stock market opened higher on Friday, as Wall Street seemed to remain unfazed by the U.S. government shutdown.

S&P 500 (SP500) +0.2%, the Dow (DJI) +0.2% and the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) +0.2%.

Wall Street climbed higher on Thursday as both the tech-focused Nasdaq Composite and benchmark S&P 500 hit new intraday record highs as the government shutdown hit a second day.

"In terms of the shutdown, yesterday brought no sign of an end to the impasse, with investors becoming increasingly concerned that it could drag on into next week," Deutsche Bank's Jim Reid said. 

The shutdown came into effect at midnight on Wednesday after Republicans and Democrats failed to reach a federal funding deal. Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent on Thursday told CNBC that U.S. GDP growth could take a hit from the shutdown.

Wall Street has largely shrugged off the shutdown. On the prediction market Kalshi, the odds of the length of the shutdown lasting more than 10 days ticked up to 69%. 

Due to the closure, market participants will be without the September nonfarm payrolls report. The data was widely seen as a key report for the Federal Reserve to assess the labor market situation. 

"Indeed, we’d normally be previewing the payroll numbers this morning, but the shutdown has seen that postponed, along with the usual weekly jobless claims yesterday," Reid added. 

Other economic data, like the September PMI Composite Final and the September ISM Services Index, will land during market hours. The former is expected to come in at 53.6 and the latter at 51.6.

By the numbers

Treasury yields were modestly higher across the curve. The 2-year yield rose 1.6 basis points to 3.56%, the 5-year climbed 2.7 bps to 3.69%, and the 10-year added 1.3 bps to 4.10%. The 30-year yield inched up 0.5 bps to 4.70%.

Gold rose $27.66 to $3,883.78, up 0.72%. Silver gained $0.65 to $47.64, a 1.38% increase, while crude oil advanced $0.40 to $60.88, up 0.66%.

data.title: Youxin Technology regains compliance with Nasdaq stockholders’ equity rule
data.description:
• Youxin Technology (NASDAQ:YAAS) on Friday said that it has received a formal notice from the Listings Qualifications Department of The Nasdaq Stock Market LLC (“Nasdaq”) on October 2, 2025, confirming that the Company has regained compliance with Nasdaq stockholders’ equity rule under Listing Rule 5550(b)(1), which requires listed companies to maintain a minimum of $2.5 million in stockholders’ equity.
  • Previously, on August 13, 2025, the Company received formal notice from the Listings Qualifications Department of The Nasdaq Stock Market LLC confirming its compliance.
  • On September 30, 2025, the Company submitted evidence to Nasdaq demonstrating stockholders’ equity exceeding $2.5 million, prompting Nasdaq to close the matter.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: AI Infrastructure Acquisition prices upsized $120 million initial public offering
data.description:
• AI Infrastructure Acquisition Corp (AIIA) announced on Friday the pricing of its initial public offering of 12 million units at an offering price of $10.00 per unit, with each unit consisting of one class A ordinary share and one right.
  • Each right entitles the holder to receive one-fifth (1/5) of one Class A ordinary share upon consummation of the company’s initial business combination, said the company. 
  • The units are expected to be listed on the New York Stock Exchange (NYSE) and trade under the ticker symbol “AIIAU” beginning October 3, 2025.
  • Once the securities comprising the units begin separate trading, the Class A ordinary shares and the rights are expected to be traded on the NYSE under the symbols “AIIA” and “AIIAR,” respectively. AIIA Sponsor, a Cayman Islands ordinary resident company, limited by shares, is a minority-owned subsidiary of Jet.AI (JTAI).
  • Maxim Group LLC is acting as sole book-running manager for the offering.
  • The company has granted the underwriters a 45-day option to purchase up to 1.8 million additional units at the initial public offering price to cover over-allotments, if any.
  • The offering is expected to close on October 6, 2025.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Ovid rises after positive results from epilepsy study
data.description:
• Ovid Therapeutics (NASDAQ:OVID) stock rose after encouraging results from its early stage study testing its experimental candidate, OV329, aimed at treating drug-resistant epilepsy.
  • Stock is up 24% premarket.
  • The results of the study showed that OV329 significantly inhibits GABA-AT, as evidenced by various measurements.
  • In the study, OV329 demonstrated a favorable safety and tolerability profile, with all potentially treatment-related adverse events reported as mild and transient.
  • Looking ahead, Ovid plans to move OV329 into a Phase 2a trial targeting drug-resistant focal onset seizures.
  • Additionally, the company is set to complete a final study to support regulatory submissions for its first oral KCC2 direct activator, OV4071, anticipated in early 2026.

data.title: CleanSpark produces 629 bitcoins in September
data.description:
• CleanSpark (NASDAQ:CLSK) said on Friday it has produced 629 bitcoins in September, a drop of 4% from 657 bitcoins produced in August 2025.
  • Throughout August 2025, the company's average hashrate was 45.6 EH/s.
  • Peak efficiency of deployed fleet was 16.07 J/Th, resulting in an average of 20.95 bitcoin mined daily.
  • The single day high reached 21.71 bitcoin.
  • The company sold 444.95 bitcoin during September 2025 at an average price of approximately $109,568 per bitcoin.
  • As of September 30, 2025, CleanSpark's Bitcoin holdings were 13,011 in total.
  • CLSK +2.97% premarket to $15.59.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Virtus Diversified Income & Convertible Fund announces redemption of class A mandatory redeemable preferred shares
data.description:
• The Virtus Diversified Income & Convertible Fund (NYSE:ACV) (NYSE: ACV) on Friday announced the redemption of all 1,200,000 issued and outstanding Class A Mandatory Redeemable Preferred Shares (Class A MRP Shares) (CUSIP No. 01883J2#34).
  • The Class A MRP Shares will be redeemed at a cash price of $25.00 per share, plus any accrued and unpaid dividends (whether or not declared) up to, but not including, the redemption date.
  • This redemption follows the mandatory redemption date of October 2, 2025, and is being funded through a master margin loan agreement with a commercial bank.
  • Upon redemption, the Class A MRP Shares shall cease and terminate.
  • ACV -0.08% premarket to $24.2.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Riot Platforms sees 7% dip in September BTC production
data.description:
• Riot Platforms (NASDAQ:RIOT) produced 445 BTC in September 2025, down 7% from August, but up 8% year-over-year.
  • Average daily BTC production declined 4% month-over-month to 14.8 BTC/day.
  • Riot sold 465 BTC in September, generating $52.6M in net proceeds.
  • Average selling price per BTC was $113,043, down 2% from August.
  • Riot held 19,287 BTC at month-end, including 3,300 restricted BTC.
  • Deployed hash rate remained flat at 36.5 EH/s; operating hash rate rose 3% to 32.2 EH/s.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: PayPal cut to Peer Perform at Wolfe on branded growth uncertainty
data.description:
Wolfe Research downgraded shares of PayPal Holdings (NASDAQ:PYPL) to Peer Perform from Outperform, citing concerns that momentum in the company’s branded checkout business may take longer to materialize than investors expect.

Shares slipped -0.9% premarket.

Analyst Darrin Peller said that while management has made progress on initiatives such as modern checkout, Venmo integration and capital discipline, questions remain about PayPal’s ability to deliver consistent branded growth of around 7%. Wolfe highlighted that PayPal is guiding for 5% branded growth in the third quarter, with headwinds from softer consumer spending in key regions and pressure in Germany, its largest European market.

“Shares may remain rangebound until PYPL demonstrates its ability to consistently deliver branded growth, even as we acknowledge relatively sound EPS growth,” Peller wrote. He trimmed his year-end 2026 fair value estimate to a range of $70-$80, from a prior price target of $85.

The analysts said PayPal’s medium-term target of 8% to 10% branded growth by 2027 remains a “show-me story,” noting that penetration of modern checkout has yet to meaningfully move the needle. Growth in “Pay with Venmo” and buy now, pay later services will also require significant expansion to hit targets, amid rising competition.

Still, Wolfe pointed to potential upside catalysts including faster-than-expected branded acceleration, improved capital returns such as a dividend, and new initiatives in agentic commerce and stablecoins.

data.title: 4 stocks to watch on Friday: AAPL, CVX, CBRL, JNJ
data.description:
Stock index futures were higher on Friday, as Wall Street is hoping to remain unfazed by the U.S. government shutdown. Here are the stocks to watch on the day:

Apple (AAPL) shares fell 1.2% in premarket trade as Jefferies downgraded the stock to Underperform from Hold citing excessive expectations on iPhones and foldable phones. The firm decreased the price target on the stock to $205.16 from $205.82. The analysts noted that demand for iPhone 17 started out only lukewarm but has grown over time.

Chevron (CVX) stock was largely flat before the opening bell as police said a massive fire that broke out at Chevron's (NYSE:CVX) El Segundo refinery near Los Angeles late Thursday has been contained. It is not yet clear what caused the explosion at the facility, which supplies jet fuel for the Los Angeles International Airport and supplies 20% of all motor vehicle fuels and 40% of the jet fuel consumed in Southern California.

Cracker Barrel (CBRL) shares were 1% higher in extended trade as the firm ended its relationship with the marketing firm it hired to refresh its logo and soften its rustic identity. It has ended its engagement with Prophet, the consultancy that advised the company on its previous brand refresh initiatives, which included new menu offerings as well as store and restaurant redesigns. The most controversial, however, was the new Cracker Barrel logo, which removed the familiar old man and barrel for a “cleaner version.”

Johnson & Johnson (JNJ) rose 0.8% in premarket trade as Wells Fargo upgraded the firm to overweight from equal weight, saying that the healthcare giant is attractively priced with pharmaceutical tariff risks and drug pricing concerns in the rearview mirror. The bank upped its price target to $212 from $170.
